reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio with high content accuracy and clear pronunciation (0.0s-6.5s). Regarding the core paralinguistic requirement 'Rhythm' (节奏), the model successfully initiates the speech with a rapid, urgent pace (0.0s-2.5s), effectively conveying the tension of the match. However, it fails to execute the required transition to a 'broken/staccato' (断续) style in the latter half. From 2.5s to the end ('快传球...'), the rhythm remains consistent with the opening—fast and fluid—without the necessary pauses, hesitation, or breathless quality that 'broken' implies. The model ignores the dynamic adjustment instruction, resulting in a static paralinguistic delivery.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates excellent content accuracy and natural fluency. Crucially, it succeeds in the 'Dynamic Adjustment' of the rhythm. The audio begins with a very fast, energetic pace (0.0s-2.8s) for the commentary ('比赛只剩下...'), matching the 'rapid' instruction perfectly. At 2.9s, there is a distinct and natural shift in prosody; the subsequent lines ('快传球，射门……') are delivered with a staccato rhythm, incorporating audible breaths and short, sharp pauses between phrases. This effectively mimics the 'broken' speaking style requested. The transition is smooth yet noticeable, fulfilling the core evaluation criteria.
model_comparison: Both models accurately synthesize the text and maintain high intelligibility. The decisive difference lies in the execution of the paralinguistic command 'Dynamic Adjustment' regarding rhythm. Model 1 (T1) maintains a 'Rapid' pace throughout the entire clip, failing to switch styles. Model 2 (T2) successfully implements the instruction: it starts 'Rapid' and clearly switches to 'Broken/Staccato' at the appropriate semantic turning point. T2 demonstrates a superior capability to control prosodic features dynamically within a single generation, whereas T1 produces a flat (style-wise) generation.
